title: Stopping an executing workflow after deletion
symptom: After deleting a workflow, it continues to show as 'Executing' in n8n. Users are unable to stop the execution through normal means.
not_this_if: Workflow is still active and not deleted
root_cause: When a workflow is deleted in n8n, it may not immediately stop any ongoing executions. This can lead to confusion as the workflow appears to be still running even after deletion.
fix_steps: Navigate to the workflow page in n8n
Click on the 'Archive' option for the workflow
Select 'Permanently delete' to stop all executions related to that workflow
agent_instruction: Archive the workflow and then permanently delete it to stop the execution.
safety_notes: Permanently deleting a workflow cannot be undone.
